content:
Pheonix stood her ground even as the white man pointed a gun in Pheonix’s face. On page 7, the last paragraph of the story it reads, “The man came back, and his own dog panted about them. “Well, I scared him off that time,” he said, and then he laughed and lifted his gun and pointed it at Pheonix. She stood and straight faced him”. This quote explains the intimidation and fear that he was trying to instill upon Pheonix.

On page 3 of the short story she is walking on the path that has bushes and thorns, and she gets caught on it it reads “Her fingers were busy and intent, but her skirts were full and long, so that
before she could pull them free in one place, they were caught in another. It
was not possible to allow the dress to tear. “I in the thorny bush,” she said.
“Thorns, you doing your appointed work. Never want to let folks pass, no sir.
Old eyes thought you was a pretty little green bush.”. This quote symbolizes that Pheonix was facing some challenges on the path. Pheonix thought the bush was pretty and harmless but became indifferent once if began to take a hold of her dress and tear it she speaks to the bush to let it know that they have succeeded its job on her dress.
